page: Amazon Web Services m8idb.24xlarge is a general-purpose, eighth-generation virtual machine instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs built on the x86_64 architecture using Intel processors running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The system provides 48 physical cores with 2.0 threads per core, paired with 384.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core allocation.

For storage-intensive workloads, the instance includes 5700 GB of local NVMe SSD instance store volumes. This high-capacity local storage is bundled directly with the instance, offering low-latency, high-throughput data access. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 100 Gbps, facilitating rapid data transfer and high-throughput communication for distributed environments.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the integration of high-speed local NVMe storage alongside substantial compute and memory resources reduces the need for external block storage attachments, optimizing resource density for data-heavy applications. The lack of a GPU makes it less suitable for accelerated machine learning training, but the combination of 96 vCPUs, 100 Gbps networking, and 5.7 TB of local NVMe SSDs makes it highly effective for databases, large-scale web serving, CI/CD pipelines, and general-purpose enterprise workloads.
description: Amazon Web Services m8idb.24xlarge is an x86_64 general-purpose instance featuring 96 dedicated Intel vCPUs operating at 3.9 GHz, managed by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It is equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core. A key hardware feature is the inclusion of 5700 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, offering high-speed, low-latency local data access without requiring external network-attached storage. Additionally, the instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps. This hardware profile makes the m8idb.24xlarge highly cost-efficient for resource-dense deployments that require a balance of compute, memory, and high-performance local storage. It is well-suited for enterprise databases, high-throughput web serving, and data-intensive general-purpose workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8idb.24xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 96 Intel vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, 5700 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth running on the Nitro hypervisor.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8idb.24xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 96 Intel vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, 5700 GB local NVMe SSD, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A high-capacity general-purpose platform featuring ninety-six dedicated Intel vCPUs, baseline one-hundred Gbps networking, and five-point-seven terabytes of local NVMe SSD storage.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated Intel vCPUs running at 3.9 GHz on x86_64 architecture
384.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced resource ratio
5700 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD instance storage
100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput data transfer
Powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor for dedicated resource allocation
Optimized for enterprise databases, web serving, and data-intensive workloads
